David committed himself to finding a place for the Lord, for the ark, the token of God’s presence. When work is to be done for the Lord, it is good to commit ourselves to a time. It is good in the morning to decide on work for the day, with submission to Providence, for we do not know what a day may bring forth. And we should first, and without delay, seek to have our own hearts made an habitation of God through the Spirit. He prays that God would take up his dwelling in the habitation he had built; that he would give grace to the ministers of the sanctuary to do their duty. David pleads that he was the anointed of the Lord, and this he pleads as a type of Christ, the great Anointed. We have no merit of our own to plead; but, for His sake, in whom there is a fullness of merit, let us find favor. And every true believer in Christ is an anointed one, and has received from the Holy One the oil of true grace. The request is that God would not turn away, but hear and answer their petitions for his Son’s sake.

The Lord never turns from us when we plead the covenant with his anointed Prophet, Priest, and King. How vast is God’s love for people that he should speak thus concerning his church! It is his desire to dwell with us; yet how little do we desire to dwell with him! He abode in Zion until the sins of Israel caused him to give them up to the spoilers. Do not forsake us, O God, and deliver us not in like manner, sinful though we are. God’s people have a special blessing on common enjoyments, and that blessing puts peculiar sweetness into them. Zion's poor have reason to be content with a little of this world because they have better things prepared for them. God will abundantly bless the nourishment of the new man and satisfy the poor in spirit with the bread of life. He gives more than we ask, and when he gives salvation, he will give abundant joy. God will bring to nothing every design formed to destroy the house of David until King Messiah should arise out of it to sit upon the throne of his Father. In him all the promises center. His enemies, who will not have him to reign over them, shall at the last day be clothed with shame and confusion forever.
